soliton.text: POLICY_SOME_HTML, POLICY_NO_HTML, and HTML_TAGS are immutable constants but are declared as instance fields instead of static final, causing unnecessary per-instance rebuilding and inconsistency with ANCHOR_PATTERN

verifySafeHtml wraps IOExceptions in raw RuntimeException instead of throwing MojoExecutionException, resulting in unstructured Maven plugin failures and poorer error messages (e.g., missing/unreadable English source file path not reported cleanly)
AccessTokenContext constructor mistakenly null-checks grantType twice, so rawTokenId is never validated and can be stored as null, producing malformed/colliding token IDs like "...:null"
AssertEvents.isAccessTokenId matcher uses the wrong substring offset (substring(3,5)), slicing across tokenType/grantType boundary and extracting the wrong grant shortcut

AssertEvents.isAccessTokenId matcher has an inverted condition that returns false when the grant shortcut matches the expected value, causing tests to pass for wrong prefixes and fail when encoder is correct
OAuth2GrantTypeFactory adds a new non-default abstract method getShortcut(), which will cause AbstractMethodError at startup for third-party implementations compiled against the old interface

DefaultTokenContextEncoderProviderFactory.postInit unconditionally casts provider factories to OAuth2GrantTypeFactory and calls getShortcut(), amplifying startup breakage for non-updated/out-of-tree grant factories
encodeTokenId behavior breaks token issuance for grants with null/unregistered shortcuts by throwing IllegalStateException("Cannot encode token with unknown grantType") instead of falling back
Constants.GRANT_TYPE may not be set on clientSessionCtx for authorization-code flow, causing grantType to fall back to UNKNOWN ("na") and encoding access tokens as onrtna:UUID instead of onrtac:UUID
Integration tests provide no real coverage of the new grant-type encoding because the broken isAccessTokenId matcher lets assertions pass even when the grant shortcut is wrong

DefaultTokenContextEncoderProviderFactory lazy refresh in getShortcutByGrantType/getGrantTypeByShortcut performs non-atomic updates across two ConcurrentHashMaps, allowing inconsistent half-populated state under concurrency/hot-deploy scenarios

RecoveryAuthnCodeInputLoginBean calls Optional.get() on the result of RecoveryAuthnCodesUtils.getCredential(user) without checking presence, risking NoSuchElementException and a 500 during Freemarker rendering when the credential is missing

CredentialHelper.createRecoveryCodesCredential catches IOException from JsonSerialization.writeValueAsString and rethrows a generic RuntimeException without a descriptive message or Keycloak-specific context, leading to opaque 500s and harder log/audit triage

Changing RECREATE_UPGRADE_EXIT_CODE from 4 to 3 is backwards-incompatible and will break external scripts/pipelines that interpret exit code 4 as 'recreate upgrade required' (it now means 'feature disabled')

The exit-code renumbering is documented/contracted in existing docs, so changing it without preserving the value or clearly calling it out in release notes/upgrade guidance risks silent behavior changes for preview adopters

operator/scripts/Dockerfile-custom-image unconditionally enables the preview rolling-updates feature via --features=rolling-updates, forcing all downstream custom images to opt into the preview feature even if they don’t use it

operator/src/test/.../UpgradeTest.java removes UnsupportedSpec initialization while adding FeatureSpec initialization, mixing an unrelated semantic change into the feature-flag update and potentially altering the test’s exercised code path
hasPermission(client, scope) falls back to a type-level resource via a side-effecting getOrCreate call, mutating the authorization store during a read-only permission check

hasPermission(client, scope) constructs a ResourcePermission with all type-level scopes instead of only the requested scope, which can lead to fragile/incorrect evaluations (e.g., false positives under future policy/evaluation changes)

ClientRemovedEvent listener calls clients().setPermissionsEnabled(..., false) under V2, but ClientPermissionsV2.setPermissionsEnabled throws UnsupportedOperationException, crashing client deletion event handling
RoleRemovedEvent and GroupRemovedEvent cleanup paths may also crash under V2 if their V2 permissions implementations similarly throw UnsupportedOperationException on setPermissionsEnabled

Admin client REST endpoints for getPermissions/managePermission/viewPermission/configurePermission crash with 500 under V2 because ClientPermissionsV2 overrides these methods to throw UnsupportedOperationException

Token-exchange permission checks (canExchangeTo and exchangeToPermission) throw UnsupportedOperationException under V2 instead of failing closed (deny/return null), causing 500s and potentially unsafe behavior depending on caller handling

Per-client permissions toggle endpoints (isPermissionsEnabled and setPermissionsEnabled) crash with 500 under V2 because ClientPermissionsV2 throws UnsupportedOperationException, breaking the admin console Permissions tab

getClientsWithPermission returns resource names that are client UUIDs (client.getId) rather than clientId, which can break callers that compare against ClientModel.getClientId and silently deny access

Entity-removal cleanup listener is now gated behind Profile.Feature.ADMIN_FINE_GRAINED_AUTHZ; if this flag corresponds to V2 only (or is disabled), V1 fine-grained-authz cleanup may stop running and leak orphaned authorization resources/policies on deletions
GroupPermissionsV2.canManage() incorrectly treats VIEW scope as sufficient for manage rights, enabling privilege escalation (e.g., creating top-level groups)
Test coverage gap: GroupResourceTypeEvaluationTest.testManageAllGroups only tests MANAGE happy path and does not catch the VIEW→manage regression
GroupPermissionsV2.canManage() implementation violates its documented contract (“permission to MANAGE groups”) by allowing broader access than described
Removing the Java-level .filter(usersEvaluator::canView) from user search can exclude users granted via per-user FGAP permissions when relying only on group-based SQL filtering

New user search behavior can return incorrect results: if no viewable groups, users with direct per-user VIEW/MANAGE may be filtered out; if groupIds is empty and SQL filter is skipped, results may be unfiltered without the post-filter
Same user-search filtering regression pattern is also applied in BruteForceUsersResource.searchForUser

AdminPermissionsSchema.getOrCreateResource now throws IllegalStateException for unknown resource types, changing error handling and likely producing 500 responses instead of prior model/not-found style errors
GroupPermissionEvaluator javadoc for getGroupIdsWithViewPermission is incorrect: claims it returns a Stream but the method returns Set<String>
GroupPermissionEvaluator javadoc under-specifies empty-set semantics for getGroupIdsWithViewPermission, potentially misleading readers about access vs “no filter required” meaning
GroupPermissionEvaluator.requireManageMembers(GroupModel) javadoc incorrectly references canManageMembership instead of canManageMembers
GroupPermissionEvaluator.requireManageMembers and requireManageMembership have duplicated/indistinguishable javadoc, making the two methods’ contracts unclear

Adding a non-default abstract method `order()` to the public `CryptoProvider` SPI breaks third-party implementations (compile failures on recompile and `AbstractMethodError` at runtime for existing binaries when `CryptoIntegration.detectProvider` calls `order()`).

`ASN1Decoder.readInteger()` passes the `readLength()` indefinite-length sentinel (-1) into `read(length)`, causing `NegativeArraySizeException` instead of an `IOException` when BER indefinite-length (0x80) is encountered.

`ASN1Decoder.readInteger()` and `readSequence()` accept non-canonical DER (e.g., superfluous leading bytes, zero-length INTEGER) and do not enforce full consumption/length matching, allowing malformed inputs and trailing bytes to be silently accepted.
`ASN1Decoder.readInteger()` allows zero-length INTEGER values, leading to `NumberFormatException` from `new BigInteger(new byte[0])` instead of a declared `IOException`.
`ASN1Decoder.readTagNumber()` has an unbounded high-tag-number continuation loop, allowing `int` overflow and potentially letting crafted tags wrap to legitimate values and bypass tag checks.

`AuthzClientCryptoProvider.concatenatedRSToASN1DER` contains dead code: two `ASN1Encoder.create().write(...)` calls whose results are discarded, doing unnecessary work and reducing maintainability/auditability.

`AuthzClient.create(InputStream)` is not updated to initialize crypto; if it does not delegate to `create(Configuration)`, callers may bypass `CryptoIntegration.init(...)` and hit runtime failures when resolving a crypto provider.
Regression test does not join the reader thread before asserting caughtExceptions is empty, so in-flight requests can add exceptions after the test passes (false-negative/flaky test)
Reader thread may exit before deletes complete due to scheduling, reducing contention and potentially missing the concurrency bug the test is meant to catch
Test does not verify the reader thread actually performed reads during the delete window, so it may not exercise the intended race

GroupAdapter.getSubGroupsCount() returns null when the underlying group model disappears, which can propagate null through the API, cause downstream auto-unboxing NPEs, or serialize subGroupCount as null
GroupAdapter behavior is inconsistent/half-fixed: getSubGroupsCount() handles missing model but sibling getSubGroupsStream(...) overrides still call getGroupModel() and remain NPE-prone
Reader thread catch block swallows exceptions without logging/stack traces, making CI failures hard to diagnose
Reader loop is a busy spin with no sleep/backoff or iteration cap, potentially overloading the server and starving deletes, hiding the intended contention
Test lacks an overall timeout; if deletes hang, the reader loop and test can hang the build until external kill
Using raw new Thread(...).start() is inconsistent with suite’s ExecutorService pattern, making exception propagation and cleanup harder
Reader thread can leak on test failure (e.g., assertion throws before deletedAll is set), potentially leaving a non-daemon thread running until JVM exit
UsernameForm.authenticate() calls a non-existent zero-argument isConditionalPasskeysEnabled(), causing a compilation/build failure (method only exists with a UserModel parameter)

UsernamePasswordForm.authenticate() now gates webauthnAuth.fillContextForm(context) behind isConditionalPasskeysEnabled(context.getUser()), suppressing passkey Conditional UI when context.getUser() is null (anonymous login/discoverable-key flow regression)

UsernamePasswordForm.challenge() condition was changed to isConditionalPasskeysEnabled(context.getUser()), effectively inverting the intended user-null logic so the passkey Conditional UI is not re-populated on error pages for anonymous users

Tests in tests/sentry/spans/consumers/process/test_flusher.py likely still reference removed/renamed SpanFlusher attributes (e.g., buffer/process/healthy_since/process_restarts), causing AttributeError/TypeError
Backpressure check loop in flusher.py raises MessageRejected on the first backpressured process, skipping remaining processes and undercounting backpressure metrics when multiple processes are stuck
In test_basic, time.sleep(0.1) is ineffective because time.sleep is globally monkeypatched to a no-op, making the test rely on thread-scheduling races and causing flakiness
Helper _create_process_for_shard in flusher.py is dead code (never called), creating a maintenance trap and potential future misuse
Flusher class docstring incorrectly claims 'one process per shard' while implementation spawns min(max_processes, num_shards) processes and distributes shards round-robin
Comment about updating healthy_since 'for all shards handled by this process' is misleading because it writes a single shared Value per process (process-level heartbeat, not per-shard)
OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_result slices a Django QuerySet with a negative start_offset when cursor.offset < 0, causing Django to raise AssertionError and crash the request
Attacker-controlled cursor offset (via ?cursor=) can repeatedly trigger the negative-slice crash, leading to 500s and potential DoS amplification (self-error/audit-log noise)
BasePaginator.get_result sets start_offset to raw offset when cursor.is_prev=True, allowing negative offsets to reach QuerySet slicing and crash with AssertionError
OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_item_key uses math.floor/ceil without importing math, causing NameError at runtime

Advanced/optimized pagination is gated by has_global_access (Owners/Managers) plus a user-controlled query param, exposing a buggy/under-tested audit-log pagination path too broadly (weak authorization/insecure design)

OptimizedCursorPaginator boundary/sentinel length check compares against raw offset instead of clamped start_offset, causing an off-by-one that can return an extra result when offset was negative and clamped
Comment in sentry/utils/cursors.py claims a performance optimization/behavior change for negative offsets, but the code is unchanged and the comment is misleading

Comment in BasePaginator/paginator.py claims negative offsets are allowed for bidirectional pagination, but forward path clamps to 0 and backward path passes negatives through (crashing), so the comment misrepresents behavior
OptimizedCursorPaginator docstring advertises negative offset support, boundary handling, and optimization that are broken or not implemented, misleading maintainers

Reducing bleve cache mutex scope in BuildIndex allows two concurrent calls with the same key to race, causing concurrent index creation on the same on-disk directory path and one bleve.New(dir, ...) to fail
With same-key concurrent BuildIndex, the cache update becomes last-writer-wins, potentially orphaning the losing bleve.Index while it continues receiving writes (file handle/disk leak)
Same-key concurrent BuildIndex can double-increment IndexMetrics.IndexTenants for a single key

In the memory-only BuildIndex branch (size <= FileThreshold), same-key concurrent calls both build full indexes and invoke builder() (duplicate backend reads/work), and one result is silently discarded

Reordering initialization so watcher subscription happens after search.init creates an event-loss window where mutations between the snapshot RV and watcher subscription are dropped, leaving the in-memory index stale until a rebuild

MetricAlertDetectorHandler now subclasses StatefulDetectorHandler but does not implement required abstract methods (get_dedupe_value, get_group_key_values, build_occurrence_and_event_data), making it uninstantiable and causing runtime TypeError when handlers are materialized

PriorityLevel(new_status) implicitly couples DetectorPriorityLevel to PriorityLevel by matching integer values, risking ValueError and hidden breakage if either enum changes; conversion should use an explicit mapping

Switching evaluate to return a dict removes/weakens duplicate group-key diagnostics: duplicates can be silently overwritten (e.g., if evaluate_group_key_value returns a different group_key or custom handlers build dicts), and the prior duplicate-detection test was deleted

Test test_state_results_multi_group uses a mismatched mock value for group_2 (passes 6 instead of 10), currently hidden because the helper ignores the value parameter and may confuse/fail future value-dependent assertions

Both new modules (src/sentry/monitors/logic/incidents.py and src/sentry/monitors/logic/incident_occurrence.py) define a module-level logger that is never used, leaving dead code (and potentially an unnecessary logging import)

Refactor renames/moves previously public symbols from sentry.monitors.logic.mark_failed without providing backward-compatible aliases, which can break external/internal imports with ImportError (e.g., mark_failed_threshold, create_issue_platform_occurrence, SimpleCheckIn, and other helpers/constants)
Dataclass field `queued = timezone.now()` is evaluated at import time, so all `AssignmentSource` instances share a stale timestamp instead of getting a per-instance value
`AssignmentSource.to_dict()` uses `asdict()` which leaves `queued` as a raw `datetime`, causing Celery JSON serialization to fail (or to round-trip as a string and corrupt the type)

`installation.sync_assignee_outbound(...)` is called with `assignment_source=` but the abstract `sync_assignee_outbound` signature (and likely concrete overrides) does not accept this kwarg, causing `TypeError` on assignee sync

`installation.should_sync("outbound_assignee", parsed_assignment_source)` passes a new second positional argument; concrete overrides that still take only one argument will raise `TypeError` (and ignoring it can break cycle-prevention)
Celery task `sync_assignee_outbound` adds new kwarg `assignment_source_dict`, which is backwards-incompatible during rolling deploys because old workers will error on unexpected kwarg
`AssignmentSource.from_dict` silently returns `None` on deserialization errors without logging/metrics, which can disable the cycle-break logic with no observability
Abstract `sync_status_outbound` signature was widened to include `assignment_source`, but concrete overrides may not accept it; they will `TypeError` once callers start passing the kwarg
Tests for `AssignmentSource.from_dict` only assert `None` on invalid input and do not assert any observability (e.g., warning log), so regressions in logging/diagnostics would go unnoticed
A single shared result_processor instance is invoked concurrently by multiple OrderedQueueWorker threads without a documented thread-safety guarantee, risking races on per-instance state

OffsetTracker.get_committable_offsets assumes strictly contiguous offsets and will stall commits forever when legitimate Kafka offset gaps occur (e.g., transactional markers or log compaction), causing unbounded lag growth

OffsetTracker retains per-partition entries (partition_locks/all_offsets/last_committed) across partition revocations and never removes them on rebalance, leading to unbounded growth/leaks in long-lived consumers
WorkItem includes a message field that is never read, unnecessarily retaining references to full Kafka payloads for queued items and increasing memory usage under backlog
OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_result slices a Django QuerySet with a negative start_offset when cursor.offset < 0, triggering AssertionError (negative indexing not supported) and crashing the endpoint
BasePaginator.get_result allows negative start_offset on the cursor.is_prev path due to clamping only the forward path, causing negative QuerySet slicing and breaking all paginator subclasses
_load_segment_data removed the max_segment_spans cap, eliminating the Python-side DoS guard and allowing unbounded span accumulation that can OOM the flush worker
Lua add-buffer script performs an unbounded ZPOPMIN (span_count - 1000) inside Redis Lua, which can block the single-threaded Redis instance under load and cause cluster-wide stalls

Redis schema change from Set ops (SADD/SCARD/SUNIONSTORE/SSCAN) to Sorted Set ops (ZADD/ZCARD/ZUNIONSTORE/ZSCAN) has no migration/compat plan, so pre-deploy keys will raise WRONGTYPE and break ingestion/flush paths
Consumer reads val["end_timestamp_precise"] as a required field; older Kafka messages lacking it will raise KeyError and stall partition processing/offset commits during rolling deploys or replays

enable_advanced_features flag passed via self.paginate is not forwarded/handled by Endpoint.paginate/paginator construction, so the flag is silently dropped or may raise TypeError, making the optimized paginator path ineffective or causing 500s
Opt-in gate uses organization_context.member.has_global_access (broader than admin), allowing non-admin members to trigger the new paginator/code path on a security-sensitive audit logs endpoint
Audit logs paginator selection is controlled by a user-tunable query parameter (optimized_pagination=true), which should not influence behavior on a security-sensitive endpoint

Lua script’s span_count logic relies on zunionstore return value plus a conditional zcard fallback; ordering/branching may cause eviction to not trigger correctly on the set_key-only path and should be verified or simplified
organization_context.member may be None; accessing organization_context.member.has_global_access can raise AttributeError for non-member callers (e.g., scoped tokens), causing a 500
Cache key uses Python built-in hash() for project_ids, so PYTHONHASHSEED randomization makes cache entries per-process and prevents cross-worker cache hits
invalidate_upsampling_cache cannot reliably delete cache entries created by other processes because it recomputes a different hash-based cache key and silently no-ops

transform_query_columns_for_error_upsampling rewrites count() to 'upsampled_count() as count', injecting SQL-style aliasing into y_axes/selected_columns that likely breaks Sentry's column parser and query execution
Using 'upsampled_count() as count' can create duplicate column names if count() appears more than once in query_columns

_set_sample_rate_from_error_sampling uses bare 'except Exception: pass' around dict.get() access, swallowing real bugs (e.g., normalized_data not a dict or unexpected non-dict contexts) and causing silent failures in tests
_set_sample_rate_from_error_sampling uses bare 'except Exception: pass' around float() conversion, silently dropping malformed client_sample_rate values instead of failing loudly
Guard 'if client_sample_rate:' treats 0.0 as falsy and skips setting normalized_data['sample_rate'], losing a valid sample rate edge case
invalidate_upsampling_cache duplicates cache-key construction logic instead of using a shared helper, risking drift that would make invalidation silently ineffective

Permission and denial caches share the same key prefix/backing store, allowing string key collisions (especially when fields contain underscores) that can silently evict/replace entries and reduce hit rate; denial keys should use a distinct prefix

`permissionCacheUsage` metric is incremented as a miss when the permission cache was hit but the cached permissions did not allow, distorting cache hit-rate metrics; should record a separate hit-with-fallthrough outcome

Denial-cache key space is attacker-controlled and unbounded because it embeds unnormalized/unbounded `Name` and `ParentFolder`, enabling low-cost in-memory amplification unless the local cache enforces max-size/eviction limits

Missing regression test for the path where cached permissions are present but do not include the requested resource, requiring DB fallthrough; test should assert DB lookup occurs and denial cache is not populated

In-process authz client behavior changes from `LocalCache(30s)` to `NoopCache`, potentially increasing DB query rate and affecting downstream expectations; should be documented as a breaking/observable change (e.g., changelog/PR notes)
get_user_info() HTTP request errors (non-200/HTTPError) are unhandled, causing a 500 instead of returning the graceful error() page
Direct access to integration.metadata["sender"]["login"] can raise KeyError when metadata/sender/login is missing, causing an unhandled 500
safe_urlopen() for OAuth token exchange is executed outside the intended try/except, so network/SSL/timeout exceptions can propagate as unhandled 500s
Token-exchange parsing uses a bare except that silently swallows all exceptions by setting payload = {} with no logging/telemetry, making failures indistinguishable operationally
Fresh-install path when Integration.objects.get raises DoesNotExist immediately advances pipeline.next_step(), bypassing the new user-mismatch validation/security check
Two Integration.objects.get calls with different status filters (any vs status=ACTIVE) can block reinstalls for DISABLED integrations by falling into a generic error path
Using two separate Integration.objects.get queries introduces a TOCTOU race where integration status/deletion can change between queries
OAuthLoginView failure paths return generic error() without any logging/metrics/Sentry events, making OAuth failures operationally invisible
Tests hardcode the pipeline state string in multiple places, making them brittle and likely to fail confusingly if pipeline signature derivation changes
Integration test cleanup uses literal alias "alias" instead of the dynamically created IDP alias, so created IDPs are not removed and leak across runs
Same cleanup bug also affects the separately created "idp-alias-20" entry, leaving it undeleted
Global getLoginPredicate() was changed to exclude org-linked private IDPs unless BROKER_PUBLIC is true, silently changing behavior for all callers beyond the new cache path
InfinispanIdentityProviderStorageProvider.remove() now always calls idpDelegate.getByAlias(alias), adding an extra DB roundtrip per remove and potentially hurting bulk-remove performance

Cache entry for getForLogin captures the revision token before querying the delegate, creating a race where a concurrent invalidation can be missed and stale data can be cached until the next mutation
enableSqlExpressions in pkg/expr/reader.go is tautological and always returns false (both branches return false), so the feature flag value is never meaningfully observed

enableSqlExpressions computes enabled as the negation of the SqlExpressions flag (!IsEnabledGlobally), creating inverted logic that could silently re-enable a dangerous path if someone later adds a return-true branch without fixing the negation
pkg/expr/sql_command.go calls sql.NewInMemoryDB() but may be missing the import for github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/expr/sql, causing a compile error

TablesList in pkg/expr/sql/parser.go constructs SQL via string concatenation/quote-doubling (fmt.Sprintf with rawSQL), leaving a latent SQL injection sink if the feature is later wired to a real engine
Incomplete double-checked locking: cache is read under RLock, then write lock is acquired without re-validating the cache, allowing multiple goroutines to redundantly reload and overwrite the cache
Expensive I/O (manifest load) is performed while holding the write lock, blocking concurrent readers and increasing contention under concurrency
Potential for inconsistent cache contents if multiple goroutines reload and overwrite the cache while the underlying manifest changes between reads
Cleanup ticker interval changed from 10 minutes to 1 minute in CleanUpService.Run, unintentionally accelerating all cleanup jobs and likely leftover debug code
Annotation cleanup functions emit routine progress logs at Error level (six new r.log.Error calls), which will trigger alerts and inflate log volume
Some Error-level diagnostic logs include full slices of IDs (potentially tens of thousands), producing extremely large log lines (e.g., ~250KB)
SQLite large-batch deleteByIDs builds an IN-list via repeated string concatenation with fmt.Sprintf in a loop, causing O(n²) time/memory due to repeated reallocations
cleanup_test batched InsertMulti uses require.NoError inside WithDbSession callback but always returns nil, losing the error for transaction handling and allowing commit instead of rollback
Tests explicitly assign deterministic primary keys (a.ID = i+1) while sharing a fakeSQL across subtests, which can cause duplicate-PK failures if subtests are later run in parallel
fetchIDs/deleteByIDs build SQL with fmt.Sprintf interpolating caller-supplied table (and condition for fetchIDs) without allowlisting/validation, inviting future SQL injection or misuse
Async legacy write goroutines derive their timeout context from the inbound request context, so they get canceled when the API handler returns and legacy writes may abort with context.Canceled
In DualWriterMode3.Create, the storage-error branch records recordLegacyDuration using the storage timer even though the legacy write was never invoked; should record storage duration instead
In DualWriterMode3.Update, the storage-error branch records recordLegacyDuration using the storage timer even though the legacy write was never invoked; should record storage duration instead

In DualWriterMode3.DeleteCollection, the async legacy goroutine records recordStorageDuration for the legacy call, double-counting storage metrics and never recording legacy metrics for delete-collection
In DualWriterMode3.Delete, klog.NewContext is given the base logger (d.Log) instead of the per-call logger with fields (name/kind/method), so downstream logs lose those fields
In DualWriterMode3.Delete, the storage-success metric uses the object name as the label instead of options.Kind, causing high-cardinality metrics and potential Prometheus/TSDB issues
DualWriterMode3.Get can panic if passed a nil *metav1.GetOptions because it dereferences options for logging and passes it downstream without a nil guard
Mode3 tests set expectations on legacy mock calls but don’t wait for the async legacy goroutine, making tests nondeterministic and potentially causing post-test failures/panics
TestMode1_Get no longer creates a fresh prometheus.Registry and instead shares a package-level registry, risking DuplicateRegistration panics and metric leakage between tests
Async legacy write failures are only logged/metriced with no retry, replay/DLQ, backpressure, or divergence detection, so legacy writes can be silently dropped and data can drift

Federated Mimir rule groups are incorrectly treated as non-federated (hardcoded `isFederated = false` in `useAllRulerRuleAbilities`), making federated rule groups appear editable/deletable even though the ruler API cannot mutate them

Grafana-managed recording rules in the list view lose the Pause, Restore, ModifyExport, and DeletePermanently actions because `useAllGrafanaPromRuleAbilities` gates these actions on `isAlertingRule`, which is false for recording rules
Rule detail links from the list view no longer include a `returnTo` parameter, removing back-navigation (“Back to list”) when navigating to `/alerting/grafana/${uid}/view`

`RuleActionsButtons` returns `null` when only a Cloud `promRule` is provided (no `rulerRule`) because it cannot construct an editable identifier, causing parent layout shifts instead of rendering an empty action container

Filtering queries by `query.expr` before template-variable interpolation can allow queries whose `expr` becomes empty after interpolation to pass through and reach shard-splitting (ordering should be interpolate then filter) in `shardQuerySplitting.ts`.
The same filter-before-interpolation ordering issue exists in `querySplitting.ts` after adding the new `applyTemplateVariables` mapping, allowing empty-after-interpolation queries to proceed.

`applyTemplateVariables` appears to be invoked multiple times (e.g., 5x for a single-target request), suggesting redundant re-interpolation downstream after `runShardSplitQuery` already interpolates once.

Repeated interpolation can incorrectly alter already-interpolated expressions when the resolved string contains literal `$` sequences that match variable names (e.g., regex/log content/ad-hoc filter values).
Repeated interpolation per shard is unnecessary work and may hurt performance; inner interpolation call sites may need removal or a guard/marker to skip if already interpolated.
CreateOrUpdateDevice performs a non-atomic count-then-insert without a transaction/locking, allowing concurrent requests to bypass the anonymous device limit (TOCTOU)

updateDevice filters by updated_at BETWEEN (now-30d) AND (now+1min), so devices last seen >30 days ago are excluded and rowsAffected=0 is misreported as ErrDeviceLimitReached, incorrectly locking out returning devices
Authenticate propagates ErrDeviceLimitReached from TagDevice, blocking anonymous authentication entirely at the device limit instead of allowing existing devices to proceed
Removal of prior panic recovery means panics inside TagDevice can now escape through Authenticate to the HTTP handler, potentially crashing the request path
Non-limit TagDevice/DB errors are only logged at WARN and authentication still succeeds, with no metric/alertable signal, so device accounting can silently fail during DB outages
tagDeviceUI errors are logged at Debug level, making failures invisible in typical production logging and reducing observability of device tagging failures
On ErrDeviceLimitReached, the local cache is not populated, causing repeated cache misses and repeated DB queries (CountDevices + updateDevice) on every request when the limit is active
AnonymousDeviceLimit is exposed in unauthenticated frontend settings, leaking configuration details that can aid attackers (information exposure)
TagDevice now uses the request context; client disconnect/cancellation can abort the DB write mid-flight, causing devices not to be recorded and weakening limit enforcement

ensure_loaded! in lib/freedom_patches/translate_accelerator.rb reads and initializes shared module-level @loaded_locales without a mutex, creating a TOCTOU race where multiple threads can concurrently decide a locale is not loaded and call load_locale
ensure_loaded! uses non-atomic `@loaded_locales ||= []` on a shared module-level variable, allowing concurrent initialization to overwrite/discard another thread’s array
ensure_loaded! adds an unsynchronized guard around load_locale even though load_locale is already idempotent under a mutex, increasing race exposure without improving safety

FallbackLocaleList#ensure_loaded! in config/initializers/i18n.rb implicitly reads I18n.locale instead of taking a locale argument, making behavior order-dependent and potentially preloading fallbacks for the previous request/default locale if I18n.locale hasn’t been set yet

TestLogger.FromContext returns a new TestLogger instead of the receiver, dropping captured log calls and breaking assertions on f.DebugLogs/InfoLogs/WarnLogs/ErrorLogs when middleware uses logger.FromContext(ctx).Info(...)
Admin EmbeddableHostsController update/destroy use `where(...).first` and then dereference nil when record not found, causing 500 instead of 404
Migration builds raw SQL INSERT with unescaped interpolation of site-setting host value, enabling SQL injection (quotes/stacked statements)

Migration indexes `execute(...)[0]['id']` without ensuring any rows returned, causing crash if embed_category setting row is missing and potentially leaving DB broken with `force: true` table creation
EmbeddableHost model `before_validation` calls `sub!` on `self.host` without nil check, raising NoMethodError when host is nil
Controller lacks strong parameters; direct `params[:embeddable_host][:host]` access crashes when key missing and permits unsafe/unvalidated input
Controller accepts `category_id` without verifying category exists or admin is authorized to use it
No uniqueness constraint/validation on `host`, allowing duplicate embeddable host entries
Store `_hydrateEmbedded` regex widened to match `_ids` keys, causing TypeError when matched field is null/undefined due to unconditional `.map`

Store `_hydrateEmbedded` now hydrates/deletes all `_ids` arrays globally (tag_ids, group_ids, etc.), breaking code expecting raw id arrays and creating undefined hydrated entries when sideloaded records are missing
TopicEmbed uses `eh.try(:category_id)` which masks failed host lookup/parse and silently falls back to default category, mis-categorizing posts without warning
PR removed `embed_category` SiteSetting fallback, reducing safety net for categorization when no host match exists
Admin embeddable-host component delete action lacks Promise `.catch`, swallowing destroyRecord failures and leaving stale UI state
Admin embedding controller `saveChanges` calls `update()` without `.then`/`.catch`, silently losing save failures and giving no user feedback
EmbeddableHost.record_for_host uses `URI(host) rescue nil`, swallowing all exceptions without logging and hiding invalid URI errors
Store code calls `self.pluralize(subType)` but Store likely has no `pluralize` method; should use `Ember.String.pluralize`, otherwise runtime TypeError
Fabricator files are swapped/inverted (category fabricators in embeddable_host file and vice versa), creating load-order fragility and maintenance confusion

Migration uses raw INSERT bypassing model normalization/validation, so scheme/path-stripping and regex validation aren’t applied, leading to stored hosts that won’t match lookups and causing silent embedding failures
Controller spec only asserts class inheritance and does not test any controller actions/paths (create/update/destroy/404/validation), providing no meaningful coverage
In rest adapter, `type.replace('_','-')` replaces only first underscore, so types with multiple underscores would normalize incorrectly and break admin model routing
Topic spec for `expandable_first_post?` passes vacuously due to default `embed_truncate` setting rather than testing EmbeddableHost-related behavior, giving false confidence
Controller fallback to `SiteSetting.uncategorized_category_id` may be nil/0, causing NOT NULL constraint violation on category_id when saving EmbeddableHost

Light-theme regression: reply link `a` color in `app/assets/stylesheets/desktop/topic-post.scss:291` changed lightness from 30% to 70%, making it much fainter than before instead of preserving the original light-theme value

Light-theme regression: `.name` color in `app/assets/stylesheets/desktop/user.scss:522` changed lightness from 30% to 50%, dimming the name and making it inconsistent with adjacent `.username a`; dark-path value also breaks the intended `100-X` pairing rule

Light-theme regression: `.custom-message-length` color in `app/assets/stylesheets/mobile/modal.scss:102` flipped from 70% to 30%, turning a subtle hint color into a high-contrast tone; appears the light/dark lightness values were transposed and now diverge from the desktop counterpart

Light-theme regression: `h3` color in `app/assets/stylesheets/mobile/topic-post.scss:182` changed lightness from 20% to 50%, weakening heading contrast and breaking parity with the desktop styling; dark-path value should follow the `100-X` rule (80%)

Light-theme regression: `.name` color in `app/assets/stylesheets/mobile/user.scss:497` changed lightness from 30% to 50%, making the name dimmer and collapsing visually with `.title`; light/dark values are inconsistent with the intended additive conversion pattern
Pagination totalPages calculation is off-by-one when user_count is an exact multiple of limit, enabling Next past the last page and producing empty results
Pagination logic should guard against zero/undefined limit to avoid incorrect totalPages computation
Admin groups controller expects params.require(:group_id) but member-scoped routes provide :id, causing ActionController::ParameterMissing at runtime
Controller specs call actions with group_id directly and don’t exercise routing, so the route/param mismatch bug is not caught; add a request/integration spec
findMembers returns undefined when group name is empty instead of a resolved Promise/RSVP, breaking callers that chain .then()
Test coverage for silently skipping non-existent usernames when adding members was removed and should be restored
Redundant group.save after modifying membership associations adds unnecessary DB update and doesn’t validate membership changes; should render success directly or adjust error handling
BlockedEmail.should_block? increments match_count/last_match_at and saves for :do_nothing rows, violating pass-through intent
BlockedEmail.should_block? performs DB writes outside the caller transaction, so counter updates can persist even if the parent transaction rolls back
BlockedEmail.should_block? silently swallows save failures, hiding persistence errors
BlockedEmail.should_block? uses read-modify-write (match_count += 1; save) causing a race condition under concurrent requests
BlockedEmail.should_block? is invoked on unauthenticated signup without rate limiting and writes on every hit, enabling write-amplification DoS
BlockedEmail.should_block? exposes an unauthenticated enumeration oracle via timing differences and structured error responses that reveal blocklist membership
email_in_restriction_setting? builds a regex from settings while only escaping '.', allowing regex injection via other metacharacters
email_in_restriction_setting? is vulnerable to ReDoS due to unescaped user-controlled/admin-controlled regex patterns causing catastrophic backtracking
email_in_restriction_setting? allows silent allow/blocklist bypass because unescaped '|' in a domain entry alters the regex alternation
email_in_restriction_setting? only treats '|' as a separator; commas/whitespace/newlines cause the entire setting to be treated as one literal and silently fail to match valid emails
email_in_restriction_setting? can raise RegexpError on malformed settings during signup, potentially causing an unhandled 500 error
Inline rescue modifier `URI(website.to_s).host rescue nil` swallows all StandardError subclasses, masking real bugs and silently returning nil in app/serializers/user_serializer.rb:137

`URI(website.to_s)` is parsed multiple times and only the first parse is guarded; subsequent `URI(...).path` calls are unguarded, redundant, and can raise inconsistently in app/serializers/user_serializer.rb:134-148

spec/serializers/user_serializer_spec.rb lacks coverage for error/edge cases (nil/empty website, malformed URIs, URIs without host, IP-address hosts), leaving current rescue/logic behavior unverified and risking misclassification (e.g., IPv4 dot-count logic)
Stored XSS risk: remote/untrusted HTML is stored with cook_method :raw_html and rendered via `<%= raw post.cooked %>` without a sanitization pipeline
Readability allowlist does not block dangerous URI schemes (e.g., `javascript:`, `data:`) in `href`/`src`, enabling XSS when rendered as raw HTML
Imported `imported_from` footer HTML is concatenated as raw markup, contributing to injection/XSS risk
`CGI.unescapeHTML` can reintroduce active markup into imported content, increasing XSS risk
`absolutize_urls` only rewrites leading-slash URLs and does not sanitize/strip dangerous schemes in `href`/`src` attributes
SSRF/local file read/command execution risk: `TopicEmbed.import_remote` uses `open(url).read` on user-influenced URLs (OpenURI/Kernel#open supports `file://` and `|cmd`)
SSRF risk: no host allowlist in `import_remote`, allowing access to internal services (e.g., metadata endpoints) even if scheme is http(s)
RSS polling path bypasses `TopicRetriever` host validation, allowing arbitrary item URLs to be fetched/imported
SSRF/local file read/command execution risk: `SimpleRSS.parse open(SiteSetting.feed_polling_url)` uses Kernel#open on an unvalidated site setting
HTML/attribute injection: i18n interpolation builds an `<a href='#{url}'>` with unescaped, user-controlled `url`, allowing quote-breaking and XSS
Unsafe postMessage usage: `targetOrigin` is built from untrusted `request.referer`, and interpolation inside `<script>` can break JS and misdirect messages
Broken access control: embed authorization relies on spoofable/absent `Referer` header host comparison
`X-Frame-Options: ALLOWALL` is not a valid/standard value and may be ignored by browsers, leaving framing protection ineffective
ERB template syntax error: `<%- end if %>` has a dangling `if` modifier and can cause template compilation failure or unexpected behavior
Job correctness/performance: `TopicRetriever#perform_retrieve` calls `Jobs::PollFeed.new.execute({})` synchronously, blocking the worker during RSS download
RSS fetch has no timeout, so a slow/hung feed can block a Sidekiq worker indefinitely
RSS polling loop does not early-exit once the requested embed URL is found, causing unnecessary work
Nil crash: `i.content.scrub` raises `NoMethodError` when RSS items lack `<content>` (i.content is nil), aborting the whole feed cycle
postMessage origin validation uses substring check (`indexOf`), allowing spoofed origins that are substrings of the expected URL
Migration safety: modifying an already-shipped migration to add `force: true` can drop and recreate the table on redo/reset/schema load, risking data loss
Disqus import robustness: `TopicEmbed.import_remote` call in `disqus.thor` is unrescued, so one dead link can abort the entire batch import
Disqus import regression: new import path drops `category_id` handling compared to previous behavior
Disqus import regression: imported topics now use current time instead of original Disqus `created_at` timestamps
Host validation bug: `TopicRetriever#invalid_host?` compares hosts case-sensitively, potentially blocking valid embeds when admin host casing differs
Duplicate `self.downsize` method definitions cause the second to overwrite the four-argument version, making the original signature unreachable and breaking callers

Public API signature change to `OptimizedImage.optimize` (and related `resize`/`downsize`) from `(width, height)` to `dimensions` can break external/in-tree callers passing five positional args, leading to wrong bindings and runtime errors
Removal of the `self.dimensions(width, height)` helper may break other code that references it
Client-side upload size validation hardcodes a 10MB cap (`maxSizeKB = 10 * 1024`) instead of using `Discourse.SiteSettings['max_' + type + '_size_kb']`, causing mismatch with configured limits
Hardcoded 10MB download cap in `uploads_controller.rb` (`FileHelper.download(url, 10.megabytes, ...)`) bypasses configurable site settings and can desync client/server limits
Upload downsizing retry loop checks `tempfile.size` after ImageMagick rewrites the file, but `Tempfile#size` may be stale due to cached stat results; should re-stat via `File.size(tempfile.path)`
After downsizing attempts, the code may not rewind the tempfile before downstream reads (e.g., `Upload.create_for`), risking incorrect reads
Downsizing loop can exhaust attempts and still proceed to upload an over-limit image because there is no rejection/fallback when the file remains above `max_image_size_kb`
Downsizing strategy uses fixed 80% reductions per attempt rather than computing a scale ratio to guarantee meeting the target size in one pass
New auto-downsize-on-upload feature lacks test coverage for oversized image uploads and for the updated `OptimizedImage.downsize/optimize` behavior
TopicsController#unsubscribe can dereference nil TopicUser (tu) when no record exists, causing 500 errors on tu.notification_level/tu.save!
TopicUser.notification_level may be nil, making notification level comparisons raise ArgumentError
Unsubscribe endpoint mutates state over GET, bypassing CSRF protections and allowing prefetch/link-scanner triggered unsubscribes
topics/unsubscribe.hbs uses triple-stash to render an i18n string interpolating model.fancyTitle without escaping, enabling stored XSS via crafted topic titles
Email unsubscribe_link translation now requires %{unsubscribe_url}, but other add_unsubscribe_link callers don’t pass it, causing MissingInterpolationArgument or broken email footers
Adding explicit render :show inside perform_show_response can cause AbstractController::DoubleRenderError for HTML topic views
Ember route topic-unsubscribe does not handle PostStream.loadTopicView promise rejections, leading to silent failures on 404/403/network errors
topic-unsubscribe route calls PostStream.loadTopicView as a static method that likely doesn’t exist, causing TypeError and breaking navigation
topic-unsubscribe controller imports deprecated discourse/controllers/object (ObjectController shim removed), breaking in modern Ember/Discourse
Ember route uses :id while Rails route/controller expects :topic_id, creating a param-name mismatch and maintenance hazard
deleteCache tRPC handler authorizes only by credentialId+userId and lacks team/org admin authorization checks, enabling broken access control/IDOR and denying legitimate team admins
deleteCache operation can be abused to force expensive calendar re-syncs (API quota burn) due to insufficient authorization/rate limiting around state-changing cache deletion
scripts/test-gcal-webhooks.sh uses a fixed predictable /tmp log file path, allowing symlink/file hijacking on multi-user systems
scripts/test-gcal-webhooks.sh interpolates untrusted $TUNNEL_URL into a sed replacement without escaping, allowing .env corruption and environment variable injection
scripts/test-gcal-webhooks.sh uses BSD/macOS-only `sed -i ''` syntax, which behaves differently/destructively on Linux
CalendarService calls updateManyByCredentialId with empty data {}, so Prisma updateMany becomes a no-op and does not bump updatedAt as intended
connectedCalendars handler directly instantiates CalendarCacheRepository instead of using the feature-flag-aware factory, bypassing the mock when the feature is disabled

Bypassing the repository factory can trigger live Prisma queries even when the calendar-cache feature is off, potentially throwing if the table/updatedAt migration isn’t present and breaking the settings page

SelectedCalendarRepository.updateManyByCredentialId has the wrong Prisma type for its data parameter (UpdateInput instead of UpdateManyMutationInput), leading to type/compile issues and misleading callers
CredentialActionsDropdown deleteCache mutation does not invalidate/refetch the connectedCalendars query after success, leaving the displayed cacheUpdatedAt timestamp stale in the UI
In working-hours validation, `end` is computed from `slotStartTime` instead of `slotEndTime`, so slots that start within hours but end after the working-hours boundary are not blocked
Dayjs instances are compared with `===` (reference equality), making the zero-duration/all-day override detection branch dead and causing all-day overrides to be ignored
UTC offset is inverted (`* -1`) before being added, shifting override times in the wrong direction and breaking date override boundary comparisons for non-UTC timezones

Using `.find()` with side-effect mutation of `dateOverrideExist` short-circuits evaluation, so multiple same-day date overrides after the first matching one are never checked and slots in later windows can be incorrectly excluded
`workingHours.find()` predicate has implicit `undefined` return paths, conflating 'different day' with 'within working hours' and making the logic semantically fragile for future changes
deleteMany OR-branch deletes reminders with retryCount > 1 without scoping to SMS method, potentially deleting non-SMS reminders
retryCount can be incremented twice when the else-branch update throws and the catch block increments again, causing premature retry exhaustion
Potential off-by-one/magic-number ambiguity in retry cleanup threshold using gt: 1 vs intended max retries; should use a named constant and correct comparator
The prisma.workflowReminder.update inside the catch block is not wrapped in its own try/catch, so if it fails the loop aborts and later reminders are skipped
Leading comment above deleteMany is stale/misleading because it mentions only past-due scheduledDate deletion and not retryCount-based deletion
No unit/integration tests cover the new retry behavior paths (cleanup on retry exhaustion, increment on null sid, increment on throw)

Public API method `InsightsBookingService.findMany` was removed and replaced with `getBaseConditions`, with no migration/update for all external callers, risking compile/runtime failures in downstream modules

Caching tests for authorization/filter condition caching were deleted while caching logic (`cachedAuthConditions`/`cachedFilterConditions` and short-circuit getters) remains, leaving cache behavior untested and prone to regressions

`getBaseConditions` contains unreachable conditional branches because `getAuthorizationConditions()` always returns a truthy `Prisma.Sql`, making the `filterConditions-only` and final `else` paths dead and obscuring the intended contract (risking future authorization bugs if nullability changes)

Constructor `options` type was widened from a discriminated union to a flat type with optional `teamId`, losing compile-time enforcement that `teamId` is required for `scope: "team"` and allowing invalid options to slip to runtime failure (`safeParse` -> `this.options` null) on a security-sensitive path

refreshOAuthTokens returns different response shapes between sync and non-sync paths (fetch Response vs GaxiosResponse-like {data}), causing callers to read res.data as undefined and breaking token refresh flows (e.g., Google Calendar bookings and other integrations)

minimumTokenResponseSchema incorrectly uses computed property keys like [z.string().toString()], creating literal keys ("ZodString"/"ZodOptional") instead of allowing extra fields, so real OAuth responses are rejected/stripped and token fields like expires_in/expiry_date can be lost
Webhook handler compares shared secret with non-constant-time !==, making it vulnerable to timing attacks
Webhook handler does not validate header type (req.headers[name] can be string[]), which can lead to incorrect secret-check behavior and type confusion
Webhook handler uses zod .parse on req.body without handling ZodError, returning unhandled 500s instead of 400 on malformed payloads
Webhook handler can throw during symmetricDecrypt/JSON.parse of keys (bad ciphertext or malformed JSON), returning unhandled 500s instead of a controlled 400 error
SalesforceCalendarService constructor performs a token refresh network call unconditionally on every instantiation, adding latency and unnecessary refreshes even when the token is still valid
Salesforce refresh logic ignores existing credential expiry_date and does not gate refresh on token expiration

Salesforce constructor throws on non-OK Salesforce responses (including transient 5xx), causing the whole service construction/event flow to fail instead of deferring/handling refresh errors gracefully
Office 365 token refresh parse-failure logging was removed, making refresh-token parsing errors silent and harder to debug
parseRefreshTokenResponse returns a safeParse result that callers don’t check for .success, so parse failures can be silently ignored
parseRefreshTokenResponse writes the literal string "refresh_token" when refresh_token is missing, poisoning stored credentials and causing subsequent refresh attempts to fail
Webhook endpoint lacks rate limiting, enabling abuse/brute force and increasing impact if the shared secret is compromised
Webhook endpoint accepts arbitrary userId in the request body and writes credentials for that user, enabling cross-user credential injection if the secret is known
Webhook endpoint should log accepted userId/appSlug actions for auditability but currently may not
parseRefreshTokenResponse uses response: any, weakening type safety; should accept unknown and rely on schema narrowing

In emailReminderManager.ts, when immediateDelete=true the code cancels the SendGrid scheduled send and returns without deleting or updating the corresponding WorkflowReminder DB row, causing orphaned reminder rows (leak)

In handleNewBooking.ts, async deleteScheduledEmailReminder/deleteScheduledSMSReminder calls are made inside forEach without await, so the outer try/catch cannot catch rejections and failures are silently ignored

In scheduleEmailReminders.ts cron, a sequential for-await loop inside a single try/catch aborts the entire batch on the first SendGrid failure, preventing subsequent DB deletions and leaving reminders stuck in cancelled:true state

In emailReminderManager.ts and smsReminderManager.ts, deleteScheduledEmailReminder/deleteScheduledSMSReminder swallow all errors (broad catch + console.log) and return void, so callers cannot detect or handle cancellation failures

In handleCancelBooking.ts, reminder cancellation promises were removed from the awaited Promise.all set; async cancellations run fire-and-forget inside forEach, allowing the API to succeed while cancellations fail or race

In workflows.tsx, using forEach(async ...) discards returned Promises so reminder cancellation calls are not awaited and errors do not propagate (also repeated at multiple forEach call sites in the file)
In bookings.tsx, bookingToReschedule.workflowReminders.forEach triggers async cancellations without awaiting; prior await Promise.all(remindersToDelete) was removed, risking incomplete cancellations

In emailReminderManager.ts, removing the (reminder.scheduled && reminder.referenceId) guard allows cron-based and inline cancellation paths to race for reminders scheduled within the next hour, potentially allowing emails to send before cron cancels them

In smsReminderManager.ts vs emailReminderManager.ts, SMS cancellation always hard-deletes the DB row while email cancellation has multiple DB outcomes (delete/mark cancelled/return), creating inconsistent cleanup semantics and future bug risk
In emailReminderManager.ts, the optional boolean immediateDelete parameter is a boolean trap that multiplexes multiple behaviors, making call sites unclear and error-prone
In scheduleEmailReminders.ts, typing an array as Prisma.Prisma__WorkflowReminderClient<...>[] leaks Prisma internal types and is unnecessary; should use a generic Promise[] type or restructure

In WorkflowStepContainer.tsx, changing the wrapper condition from (isPhoneNumberNeeded || isSenderIdNeeded) to isPhoneNumberNeeded alters UI rendering when only sender ID is needed, an unrelated and potentially unintended behavior change
Authorization check for team admin/owner uses && instead of ||, denying non-owner team admins
Authorization logic falls back to teamId ?? 0 instead of skipping team checks when teamId is missing, relying on sentinel behavior and causing unnecessary queries
Any attendee on a booking is allowed to add guests (over-broad authorization), enabling privilege bypass/IDOR-style modification of attendee list
Guests array has no upper bound in server schema, enabling DoS/mass-mail amplification via huge submissions
Incoming guest emails are not normalized (e.g., lowercased) before comparing against blacklist, allowing case-based blacklist bypass
Incoming guest emails are not normalized before duplicate-attendee checks, allowing case-based duplicates and duplicate invitation emails
Mailer is called with the original unfiltered guests list instead of the filtered uniqueGuests list, so blacklisted/existing addresses can still receive emails
sendAddGuestsEmails logic misclassifies existing attendees as “new” when their email appears in the raw input, sending the wrong email template
Input is not deduplicated within the guests array, so createMany may insert duplicates or throw unhandled Prisma P2002 without skipDuplicates
Calendar attendee update is not wrapped in error handling/compensation, so DB can commit while external calendar update fails, causing permanent desync
Email sending catch block swallows the error object and uses console.log instead of structured error logging, losing diagnostics while still returning success
sendAddGuestsEmails is imported from @calcom/emails but may not be re-exported from the package barrel (index.ts), causing runtime import failure
New email templates rendered by string key may not be registered in the renderEmail template registry/map, causing empty/failed email rendering
Client-side Zod schema enforces uniqueness but server-side schema does not, allowing duplicates to pass validation for non-UI callers
Client-side schema shape mismatches the mutation input (validates bare array vs {bookingId, guests}), making validation unreliable/bypassable
Organizer add-guests email subject uses attendees[0].name which may be empty for newly-added guests, producing blank names in subject
UI guard multiEmailValue.length === 0 is dead code due to initial state [''], so empty-input handling relies accidentally on Zod rejecting empty strings
New i18n keys were added only to en/common.json, so other locales will display raw key strings (including in email subjects)
booking.userId || 0 passed to findFirstOrThrow masks null organizer as misleading Prisma P2025 instead of a domain-level error
Backup codes are stored using reversible symmetric encryption with a single shared key instead of one-way hashes, allowing recovery of plaintext codes if DB+key are compromised
Backup code verification/consumption during login is non-atomic (read/verify then separate update), allowing the same backup code to be accepted twice under concurrent requests (TOCTOU race)
2FA disable endpoint verifies a backup code but does not consume it immediately; if disable fails after verification the code remains valid and can be retried

2FA disable endpoint’s backup-code verification is vulnerable to concurrent requests because consumption happens only at the end (or not at all), allowing the same code to disable 2FA multiple times (TOCTOU)
Backup code decrypt/parse uses unguarded JSON.parse(symmetricDecrypt(...)) so corrupt/rotated/altered data can throw untyped errors and break the recovery flow without a proper ErrorCode mapping
Clipboard copy uses navigator.clipboard.writeText without await/catch, causing unhandled promise rejections and showing a success toast even when copying fails
Backup code verification lacks rate limiting/lockout in the login authorize() path, enabling unbounded online guessing attempts once password is known
Backup code verification lacks rate limiting/lockout in the 2FA disable endpoint as well, enabling unbounded guessing attempts there too
2FA setup endpoint regenerates/overwrites backup codes even when 2FA is already enabled, silently invalidating previously saved codes and temporarily degrading 2FA state
Playwright test assertion uses expect(locator.isChecked()).toBeTruthy() without awaiting, so it always passes and masks real failures in the 2FA setup flow test
Null dereference crash when destinationCalendar is null/undefined/empty because code accesses mainHostDestinationCalendar.integration without optional chaining in packages/core/EventManager.ts:277

In Google CalendarService.updateEvent, fallback selectedCalendar uses a tautological find comparing externalId to a falsy externalCalendarId, making selectedCalendar always undefined and causing updates to target calendarId undefined in packages/app-store/googlecalendar/lib/CalendarService.ts:117

In Google CalendarService.deleteEvent, fallback calendarId uses the same tautological find with falsy externalCalendarId, making calendarId always undefined and causing deletes to target undefined; defaultCalendarId="primary" becomes dead/unused in packages/app-store/googlecalendar/lib/CalendarService.ts:128

LarkCalendarService.createEvent signature missing required credentialId parameter, violating updated Calendar interface and dropping per-host credential routing in packages/app-store/larkcalendar/lib/CalendarService.ts:137

Office365CalendarService.createEvent signature missing required credentialId parameter, violating updated Calendar interface and dropping per-host credential routing in packages/app-store/office365calendar/lib/CalendarService.ts:183

updateAllCalendarEvents may call updateEvent with credential undefined when DB lookup fails because result.push(updateEvent(...)) is outside the credentialFromDB/app.slug guard in packages/core/EventManager.ts:454

Collective team destination calendars are silently dropped when evt.destinationCalendar is initialized to null and later .push is optional-chained (no-op), breaking collective-host scenario in packages/features/bookings/lib/handleNewBooking.ts:876

Security: createAllCalendarEvents performs prisma.credential.findUnique by id without ownership/host allowlist checks, enabling cross-tenant credential use (IDOR/credential bleed) in packages/core/EventManager.ts:327
Security: updateAllCalendarEvents performs prisma.credential.findUnique by id without ownership/host allowlist checks, enabling cross-tenant credential use (IDOR) in packages/core/EventManager.ts:441

Security: handleCancelBooking falls back to prisma.credential.findUnique by id without ownership checks and uses it to delete events, enabling cross-tenant destructive actions in packages/features/bookings/lib/handleCancelBooking.ts:628

updateAllCalendarEvents catch path returns [] when an exception occurs before calendarReference assignment, making failures indistinguishable from “no calendars” and losing error visibility in packages/core/EventManager.ts:514

handleCancelBooking fallback credential fetch omits include of app.slug and does not synthesize appName, but getCalendar expects CredentialPayload with appName, leading to wrong/no integration resolution in packages/features/bookings/lib/handleCancelBooking.ts:628

Collective cancel fan-out can duplicate delete calls (N×M) when references lack credentialId, causing redundant external deletes and potential rate limiting in packages/features/bookings/lib/handleCancelBooking.ts:619

Security/privacy: Google Calendar organizer email is derived from destinationCalendar[0].externalId (arbitrary host), leaking a host’s private calendar email to attendees in packages/app-store/googlecalendar/lib/CalendarService.ts:53

Using `forEach(async ...)` in `packages/app-store/vital/lib/reschedule.ts:122` causes fire-and-forget deletes; outer try/catch won’t catch rejections and the function can finish before deletes complete

Using `forEach(async ...)` in `packages/app-store/wipemycalother/lib/reschedule.ts:122` causes fire-and-forget deletes; outer try/catch won’t catch rejections and `Reschedule()` can resolve before deletes complete

Using `forEach(async ...)` in `packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/bookings.tsx:553` makes the reschedule mutation return to the client before calendar/video deletes finish; failures can become unhandled or silently dropped

In `packages/features/bookings/lib/handleCancelBooking.ts:461`, `apiDeletes.push(...)` happens inside `forEach(async ...)`, racing with `await Promise.all(apiDeletes)` so some deletes are never awaited and failures don’t surface

In `packages/core/CalendarManager.ts:28`, `getCalendarCredentials` stores the unresolved `Promise` from `getCalendar(credential)` into the returned `calendar` field, breaking callers expecting a `Calendar | null` and causing runtime `TypeError` when methods are called

Async contract changes (`appStore[k]` now Promise, `getCalendar()` now Promise, `getVideoAdapters()` now Promise) may leave external callers un-migrated, leading to calling methods on Promises or `TypeError` (e.g., `forEach is not a function`) unless all call sites add `await`
TableWidgetVisualization feature-flag branch renders hardcoded empty columns and empty tableData, causing table widgets to appear blank for flagged orgs/users
Hook file/function renamed from useTraceItemAttributeValues to useGetTraceItemAttributeValues, so any remaining old import paths/names will cause module-not-found/runtime failures
Removal of the hook's enabled prop can trigger unintended/unconditional network requests at mount for callers that previously gated fetching
Visualize constructor signature changed from yAxes array to single yAxis string; callers still passing arrays can corrupt visualize state or fail at runtime if type checks are bypassed
Visualize.fromJSON return type changed from Visualize to Visualize[]; call sites treating the result as a single object will access properties on an array and break at runtime
Visualize.replace parameter renamed from yAxes to yAxis; callers still passing yAxes will be silently ignored, leaving stale yAxis values
UseTraceItemAttributeBaseProps type moved to types.tsx; imports from the old hook module path will fail TypeScript compilation

get_merged_pr_single_issue_template calls PRCommentWorkflow._truncate_title even though _truncate_title is defined on CommitContextIntegration; this may raise AttributeError at runtime if PRCommentWorkflow lacks the method
fetch_error_details swallows nodestore exceptions and returns an empty list, making fetch failures indistinguishable from legitimate no-errors and producing silently degraded summaries
Migrations 0917/0920 were replaced with no-ops; migration 0921 must be idempotent to handle split-state environments and partially migrated data safely
get_environment_info catches all exceptions, logs only at INFO, and returns an empty string, masking failures and producing silently incomplete PR comment template output
